title: The Caged Goldfinch
content:
Within a churchyard, on a recent grave,
     I saw a little cage
    That jailed a goldfinch. All was silence save
     Its hops from stage to stage.

    There was inquiry in its wistful eye,
     And once it tried to sing;
    Of him or her who placed it there, and why,
     No one knew anything.
meaning:
This evocative piece by Thomas Hardy, titled "The Caged Goldfinch", represents a masterful exploration of classic. The lines capture a profound emotional resonance...

### Why We Love This Line
At Linespedia, we believe that poetry is the ultimate sanctuary for the soul...
